title: core
text:
source

load_fasta

 load_fasta (fasta_path:Union[str,pathlib.Path])

Load a FASTA file and return a dictionary mapping protein IDs to sequences.









Type
Details




fasta_path
Union
Path to the FASTA file to load.


Returns
Dict
Dictionary mapping protein IDs (record.id) to amino acid sequences (as strings).




source


digest

 digest (sequence:str, protein_id:str, enzyme:str='trypsin',
         missed_cleavages:int=1, charge_states:list=[1, 2, 3],
         mass_range:tuple=(800.0, 4000.0), min_pep_length:int=5,
         max_pep_length:int=35, sort_by_mass:bool=False)

Digest a protein and add flanking amino acids for each peptide.










Type
Default
Details




sequence
str

Protein sequence to digest


protein_id
str

Protein identifier (for the DataFrame)


enzyme
str
trypsin
Enzyme name (default: ‘trypsin’)


missed_cleavages
int
1
Number of allowed missed cleavages (default: 1)


charge_states
list
[1, 2, 3]
Charge states for m/z calculation (default: [1, 2, 3])


mass_range
tuple
(800.0, 4000.0)
(min, max) monoisotopic mass filter in Da (default: (800.0, 4000.0))


min_pep_length
int
5
Minimum peptide length to retain (default: 5)


max_pep_length
int
35
Maximum peptide length to retain (default: 35)


sort_by_mass
bool
False
Sort output by monoisotopic mass (default: False)


Returns
DataFrame

DataFrame with the following columns:- start_index : int - Start position in protein sequence- end_index : int - End position in protein sequence- pep_seq : str - Peptide sequence- protein_id : str - Protein identifier- pep_length : int - Peptide length- prev_aa : str - Previous amino acid (or ‘-’ at N-terminus)- next_aa : str - Next amino acid (or ‘-’ at C-terminus)- extended_seq : str - Sequence with flanking AAs- rep_extended_seq : str - Extended sequence with parentheses- mass_mono : float - Monoisotopic mass (Da)- mass_avg : float - Average mass (Da)- mz_{z} : float - m/z for each charge state in charge_states




df = digest(
    sequence=protein_dict['P15497'],
    protein_id='P15497',
    enzyme='trypsin',
    missed_cleavages=0,
    sort_by_mass=True
  )
df.head(10)

digest_to_set

 digest_to_set (sequence:str, enzyme:str='trypsin',
                missed_cleavages:int=0,
                mass_range:tuple[float,float]=(800.0, 4000.0),
                min_pep_length:int=5, max_pep_length:int=35)

*Lightweight digest that returns only peptide sequences as a set.
This is a memory-efficient alternative to digest() when only the peptide sequences are needed, without positional or mass annotations.*










Type
Default
Details




sequence
str

Protein sequence to digest


enzyme
str
trypsin
Enzyme name (default: ‘trypsin’)


missed_cleavages
int
0
Number of allowed missed cleavages (default: 0)


mass_range
tuple
(800.0, 4000.0)
(min, max) monoisotopic mass filter in Da (default: (800.0, 4000.0))


min_pep_length
int
5
Minimum peptide length to retain (default: 5)


max_pep_length
int
35
Maximum peptide length to retain (default: 35)


Returns
set

Set of peptide sequences passing the length and mass filters




source


fasta_to_peptide_set

 fasta_to_peptide_set (fasta_path:Union[str,pathlib.Path],
                       enzyme:str='trypsin', missed_cleavages:int=0,
                       mass_range:tuple[float,float]=(800.0, 4000.0),
                       min_pep_length:int=5, max_pep_length:int=35,
                       show_progress:bool=True)

*Parse a FASTA file and return all unique peptides as a set.
Memory-efficient function that digests proteins on-the-fly without storing the full protein sequences. Useful for building canonical peptide reference sets for comparison with experimental data.*










Type
Default
Details




fasta_path
Union

Path to the FASTA file to parse


enzyme
str
trypsin
Enzyme name (default: ‘trypsin’)


missed_cleavages
int
0
Number of allowed missed cleavages (default: 0)


mass_range
tuple
(800.0, 4000.0)
(min, max) monoisotopic mass filter in Da (default: (800.0, 4000.0))


min_pep_length
int
5
Minimum peptide length to retain (default: 5)


max_pep_length
int
35
Maximum peptide length to retain (default: 35)


show_progress
bool
True
Show progress bar with tqdm (default: True)


Returns
set

Set of all unique peptide sequences from the FASTA file




peptide_set = fasta_to_peptide_set(TEST_DATA / 'test_sequence.fa')

Digesting proteins: 2it [00:00, 11765.23it/s]



source


annotate_peptides_inplace

 annotate_peptides_inplace (file_path:Union[str,pathlib.Path],
                            canonical_peptides:set[str],
                            sequence_col:str='PEP.StrippedSequence',
                            new_col_name:str='is_canonical', sep:str=None,
                            show_progress:bool=True)

Annotate peptides in-place by writing to temp file then replacing.










Type
Default
Details




file_path
Union

Path to peptide file (will be modified in-place)


canonical_peptides
set

Set of canonical peptide sequences


sequence_col
str
PEP.StrippedSequence
Column name containing peptide sequences (default: ‘PEP.StrippedSequence’)


new_col_name
str
is_canonical
Name for the new annotation column (default: ‘is_canonical’)


sep
str
None
Field separator. If None, auto-detects from extension (default: None)


show_progress
bool
True
Show progress bar (default: True)


Returns
int

Number of peptides processed




canonical = set(['DASGPAMTEIGEQPWGR', 'DVAGAVEFWTDR'])


spc_out = pd.read_csv(TEST_DATA / 'test_spectronaut_pep_out.tsv.bk',sep='\t')

text:

    config = yaml.safe_load(stream)
print(config)

title: protein_cutter
text: This file will become your README and also the index of your documentation.

title: protein_cutter
text:
Developer Guide
If you are new to using nbdev here are some useful pointers to get you started.

Install protein_cutter in Development mode
# make sure protein_cutter package is installed in development mode
$ pip install -e .

# make changes under nbs/ directory
# ...

# compile to have changes apply to protein_cutter
$ nbdev_prepare

title: protein_cutter
text:
Usage

Installation
Install latest from the GitHub repository:
$ pip install git+https://github.com/mtinti/protein_cutter.git
or from conda
$ conda install -c mtinti protein_cutter
or from pypi
$ pip install protein_cutter


Documentation
Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub repository’s pages. Additionally you can find package manager specific guidelines on conda and pypi respectively.
